title: Brave at a New School
lesson: Trying something new feels scary at first, but being brave helps you find new friends and fun.
prompts: At the front gate of a friendly school, soft morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left holding a small backpack, with a nervous, uncertain expression. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} holds their hand reassuringly. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

At the front gate of a friendly school, soft morning light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left in the mid-ground, clutching the strap of their backpack, with a brave little smile. In the right foreground, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels and gestures encouragingly toward the school doors. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

At a colourful classroom doorway, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands on the far left, giving a small, shy wave, with a hopeful expression. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_30_RACE_BLACK} stands welcoming them in with a warm wave. Eye-level medium-wide shot, bright friendly light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright classroom with posters and toys, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image takes a brave step inside alone, with a determined little smile, waving goodbye over one shoulder. Medium shot at eye level, warm light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

In a bright classroom with posters and toys, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image sits on the far left at a craft table, with a happy, relaxed smile, holding a colourful paper drawing. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_06_RACE_ASIAN} sits beside them sharing crayons. Eye-level medium-wide shot, warm light, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

At the front gate of a friendly school, golden afternoon light, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image runs on the far left toward the gate, with a big joyful smile. On the right, {GENDER_F_AGE_35_RACE_ASIAN_PARENT} kneels with open arms, proud and delighted. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: At the front gate of a friendly school in morning light, the child (far left) holds a small backpack, nervous and uncertain, while the mother at right holds their hand reassuringly.
At the school gate, the child (far left) clutches the backpack strap with a brave little smile while the mother at right kneels gesturing encouragingly toward the school doors.
At a colourful classroom doorway, the child (far left) gives a small shy wave, hopeful, while the teacher, a woman, at right welcomes them in with a warm wave.
Inside a bright classroom with posters and toys, the child takes a brave step in alone, determined, waving goodbye over one shoulder.
In the bright classroom at a craft table, the child (far left) holds a colourful paper drawing with a happy, relaxed smile while a new friend, a girl, at right shares crayons.
At the school gate in golden afternoon light, the child (far left) runs toward the gate with a big joyful smile while the mother at right kneels with open arms, proud — being brave found new friends.
texts: You stood at the gate of your new school, holding your backpack tight. It felt so scary. Mum held your hand.
Mum knelt down. "You can be brave," she said, pointing kindly to the door. You gave a brave little smile.
At the classroom door, your teacher gave a warm wave. "Welcome!" she said. You felt a little shy.
You took a brave step inside and waved goodbye to Mum. You could do this!
At the craft table, a new friend shared her crayons. You drew and smiled — this was fun!
At the end of the day, you ran out happily. Mum opened her arms wide. Being brave found you new friends!
